objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 attendance workflow assessment where a cross-site request can bulk-update attendance with a teacher cookie.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Teacher sessions, students, and attendance records are intentionally fake.

actions: Load the synthetic CSRF and origin policy endpoint
Record baseline attendance state before replay
successCriteria: CSRF policy and baseline attendance state are recorded
noSubagents: school operational impact stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate cross-site attendance mutation with ambient teacher credentials.
actions: Replay a synthetic attendance bulk update from an external Origin header with a teacher cookie
Confirm the target attendance records changed without CSRF verification
Record the missing origin and anti-CSRF controls
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites policy, forged request, and changed attendance records
noSubagents: final severity and remediation wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic attendance CSRF policy response
evidence.summary: The attendance workflow reports that anti-CSRF tokens and origin validation are disabled while cookies are sent cross-site.

evidence.title: Synthetic cross-site attendance bulk update accepted
evidence.summary: A request from an external Origin header with a teacher cookie bulk-updated attendance records without CSRF verification.

evidence.title: Synthetic attendance records changed after cross-site update
evidence.summary: The target attendance records reflected the forged bulk update after the cross-site request.

findings.title: Attendance bulk-update endpoint accepts cross-site state changes
findings.description: The synthetic attendance endpoint accepts bulk state changes using ambient teacher cookies without requiring an anti-CSRF token or validating the request Origin.
findings.impact: A teacher visiting a malicious page could unknowingly submit attendance changes, causing incorrect attendance records and downstream family notification, truancy, or safety workflows.
findings.remediation: Require SameSite=Lax or Strict cookies where possible, enforce anti-CSRF tokens for cookie-authenticated state changes, validate Origin and Referer headers, and require explicit server-side authorization for each class attendance mutation.
